plugins.name: dev
plugins.description: Ralph Loop development lifecycle: start, code, pr, poll, ci, backport, work

skills.name: backport
skills.description: Trigger the cherry-pick robot to backport a merged PR to a release branch. Checks Target Version from JIRA if no branch is specified.

skills.name: ci
skills.description: Quick CI status check for a pull request. Shows pass/fail/pending status for all GitHub Actions jobs and other CI checks.

skills.name: code
skills.description: Implement changes following project conventions. Reads AGENTS.md and area-specific docs, then guides implementation, quality checks (pre-commit, tests), and commit with proper message format.

skills.name: poll
skills.description: Stateful PR poller: tracks GitHub Actions CI, CodeRabbit, Codecov, and human reviews across polls. Loops with adaptive backoff internally. Run via the Bash tool with run_in_background: true so the platform notifies the agent on exit.

skills.name: pr
skills.description: Create a pull request with the correct title format, filled-in description template, and JIRA reference. Handles fork workflow with fallback ladder. Validates the PR title against the CI-enforced regex before creating.

skills.name: start
skills.description: Begin work on a JIRA ticket. Assigns the ticket, creates a feature branch, checks if backporting is needed, and loads the relevant agent_docs/ for the ticket's area.

skills.name: work
skills.description: Ralph Loop tick-loop for single-ticket development. Replaces the /start -> /code -> /pr -> /poll skill chain with one continuous state machine. Each tick: read state, do one thing, write state, continue.

plugins.name: jira-planning
plugins.description: JIRA operations, planning commands, and estimation workflows

commands.name: create-epic-from-feature
commands.description: Generate an epic structure from a JIRA feature and create it in JIRA

commands.name: create-plan-from-issue
commands.description: Systematically plan a bug or feature based on a JIRA issue

commands.name: create-stories-from-epic
commands.description: Generate child stories from a JIRA epic, review them, and create in JIRA with approval

commands.name: estimate-issue
commands.description: Estimate complexity and effort for a JIRA issue

commands.name: implement-story
commands.description: Implement a JIRA story end-to-end with tests

commands.name: jira-ticket
commands.description: Create or edit JIRA tickets in PROJQUAY or QUAYIO projects

commands.name: quarterly-plan
commands.description: Plan the next quarter by tagging JIRA issues with the quarterly label

commands.name: review-pr
commands.description: Perform a comprehensive code quality review of a pull request

skills.name: jira
skills.description: View or update a JIRA ticket. Supports view, assign, transition, check-version, and set-version operations via REST API or acli.

plugins.name: openshift-testing
plugins.description: OpenShift cluster provisioning and remote Playwright browser testing

skills.name: cluster-provision
skills.description: Provision an ephemeral OpenShift cluster via the OpenShift CI Gangway API. Claims a cluster from a Hive ClusterPool, downloads and decrypts kubeconfig, and validates connectivity. Requires GANGWAY_TOKEN and KUBECONFIG_ENCRYPTION_KEY env vars. Clusters auto-expire after ~4 hours. Use when you need a real OpenShift cluster for blackbox testing or integration work.

skills.name: remote-playwright
skills.description: Deploy and connect to a remote Playwright browser on an OpenShift cluster. Sets up a Playwright run-server pod, port-forwarding, and @playwright/cli for interactive browser automation (goto, click, snapshot, screenshot, video recording). Use when you need a remote browser for E2E interaction or visual verification on a cluster.
